message:
feat(recorder_migration): Cx-first run_m0_m2 composite — trail complete (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Final slice on the recorder_migration.rs Cx-first trail. Wires
the 3 previously-landed Cx-first M-stage methods into the
composite pipeline entry point.

Addition (strictly additive, asupersync-runtime-gated):

  * `MigrationEngine::run_m0_m2_with_cx(cx, source_storage,
    source_reader, target)` — Cx-first parallel of `run_m0_m2`.
    Chains all three stages through their Cx-first siblings:
      - `m0_preflight_with_cx` (tick 67) — per-batch
        checkpointed manifest scan.
      - `m1_export` (sync — no cx needed since the per-record
        loop has no await points).
      - `m2_import_with_cx` (tick 68) — per-chunk checkpointed
        write to target.
    Plus an extra boundary checkpoint between m0 and m1 to
    catch cancellation on the synchronous m1 boundary.

Why the boundary checkpoint matters: m1 is synchronous
(cursor-traversal + FNV digest with no await points), so an
internal cx.checkpoint() there would be a no-op in terms of
cancellation handling. But on large ledgers, m1 can still
take seconds-to-minutes of CPU. The boundary checkpoint lets
a caller cancel AFTER m0 completes but BEFORE the expensive
m1 burns CPU — avoiding wasted synchronous work.

Cancellation-safety: all 3 component methods' documented
contracts still apply in the composite:
  * m0 cancel: no target writes, manifest never returned.
  * m1 cancel: only fires at the boundary checkpoint (sync
    function has no internal cancel seam). If the boundary
    catches it, m1 doesn't run; if m0 catches it first, m1
    doesn't run either.
  * m2 cancel: already-committed chunks on target remain;
    batch_id idempotency means safe resume.

Regression test (new) `run_m0_m2_with_cx_matches_legacy`:

  * Runs BOTH `run_m0_m2` and `run_m0_m2_with_cx` with identical
    inputs (5-record fixture, export_batch_size=2,
    import_batch_size=3 — forces multiple batches through BOTH
    the m0 scan AND the m2 chunk loop, exercising all the
    per-batch and per-chunk checkpoint seams).
  * Separate `consumer_id` for legacy vs cx to avoid target
    batch_id collisions.
  * Asserts manifest parity across ALL 8 fields: event_count,
    first_ordinal, last_ordinal, export_count, import_count,
    export_digest, import_digest, per_pane_counts.
  * Asserts absolute invariants: event_count=5,
    import_digest==export_digest (full round-trip parity),
    target received exactly 5 events.

recorder_migration.rs Cx-first trail COMPLETE. All 4 individual
M-stage methods + the composite pipeline have Cx-first siblings:
  * `m0_preflight`       ✓ (tick 67)
  * `m2_import`          ✓ (tick 68)
  * `m3_checkpoint_sync` ✓ (tick 69)
  * `m5_cutover`         ✓ (tick 70)
  * `run_m0_m2`          ✓ (tick 71, this commit)

Verification (local fork bypass):
  * recorder_migration:: — 56 passed / 0 failed (tick 70: 55 ->
    tick 71: 56, +1 new composite parity test; all 50 legacy
    migration tests still green)
  * cargo build -p frankenterm-core --lib — clean (1m30s)
  * cargo fmt -p frankenterm-core -- --check — clean

Bead: ft-xbnl0.2.3

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
